generatedSentence: Arthur Vance, a regular man pushing fifty, found simple delight in his morning promenade through the town square, pausing to admire the ripe plum trees. Obviously, his life seemed general and predictable, cushioned by a steady payroll from his long-standing government job. He often spent holidays alone, yet found a peculiar happy contentment, allowing his imagination to roam. His apartment, though small, felt wholesome, warmed by an electric fireplace that offered ease after long days. He’d occasionally enjoy a quiet drink, reflecting on his past as an overprotected child, now a stoic man of defence. He wanted to establish a new point of latitude in his life, feeling a hollow spot. His past self was a bit dorky, the antithesis of the composed man he’d become, even though his home was filled with the scent of vanilla air freshener and his work involved mundane keypads. One morning, a rancid smell wafted from the old apartment next door, stirring an agony in his gut. He remembered the cryptic acronym he'd seen on a package delivered there. A new, peculiar neighbor, a former mixer for an underground fight club, notorious for the strange neckbands he wore, seemed to be constantly weathercocking the street. Arthur, an avid jogger, noticed a high-speed jetski often docked at the nearby lake – an undeniable pattern emerging. He felt a metaphorical plank pressing on him. His mind, like complex geometric jigsaws, struggled to connect the dots. Was this man a panther, a warrior or perhaps a modern warlock? The cold reality began to crumble his composure. He’d seen him once in a tanktop, his muscular frame hinting at a past that could easily break someone. He'd even spotted him talking on a wireless device, mentioning a 'mandatory vaccination' while his eyeball darted around like a cornered animal, hinting at illicit activities involving a private twinjet and former teammates. One night, Arthur found himself blindfolded, a flopper in a terrifying game. He'd stumbled onto a plot to skyjack a private twinjet at the local airport, a scheme that funneled funds through various shell companies, bypassing hospitals for profit. The conspirators, an unstoppable force of individuals driven by a twisted sense of evolution, spoke in hushed tones of racketeering. He felt as light as a feather as he was pushed into a waiting droshky, a vintage ride that somehow felt more menacing. His mind raced, recalling childhood nightmares now brought to life. He realized the neighbor was a witch of an operative, her methods as serpentine as her movements, and her use of high-tech drills to access secure data. It was an elaborate trick, a felony in motion, requiring finicky precision. He felt a sudden acceleration as the vehicle sped up, and knew his own injury was imminent if he didn't act, his muscular frame tense. The mention of 'rabbits' as a code word for enzymes or some illicit substance in an upcoming 'election' further solidified the danger from these jaguars, as they passed a deserted tramway station. Arthur emerged from the ordeal with a profound revelation. His past life of quiet contentment now seemed unbearable. He sought support in a therapist, facing his inner alien and the long-suppressed urge to truly live. He quit nicotine, started brewing kombucha, and found himself dating again, even if his attempts at forming new relationships sometimes felt labored. The weight of societal judgment no longer held his center as firmly. He pursued hobbies, joining an amateur basketball league, finding camaraderie with new teammates that felt almost sacred. One evening, while watching a Veejay spinning tracks at a local German beer garden, he felt an inner earthquake of change. He even toyed with the idea of leasing a small storefront to sell his homemade jaggery candies, using an ultrasound device to precisely cut the sweets. The old, overpacked anxieties began to osmose away. He was no longer the man haunted by a metaphorical line of ducklings always behind him, nor the specter of a nymphomaniac ex, but a truly transformed individual, leaving behind his vaporizer and embracing new delight.
panelSentences: Arthur Vance, a regular man pushing fifty, found simple delight in his morning promenade through the town square, pausing to admire the ripe plum trees. Obviously, his life seemed general and predictable, cushioned by a steady payroll from his long-standing government job. He often spent holidays alone, yet found a peculiar happy contentment, allowing his imagination to roam. His apartment, though small, felt wholesome, warmed by an electric fireplace that offered ease after long days. He’d occasionally enjoy a quiet drink, reflecting on his past as an overprotected child, now a stoic man of defence. He wanted to establish a new point of latitude in his life, feeling a hollow spot. His past self was a bit dorky, the antithesis of the composed man he’d become, even though his home was filled with the scent of vanilla air freshener and his work involved mundane keypads.

One morning, a rancid smell wafted from the old apartment next door, stirring an agony in his gut. He remembered the cryptic acronym he'd seen on a package delivered there. A new, peculiar neighbor, a former mixer for an underground fight club, notorious for the strange neckbands he wore, seemed to be constantly weathercocking the street. Arthur, an avid jogger, noticed a high-speed jetski often docked at the nearby lake – an undeniable pattern emerging. He felt a metaphorical plank pressing on him. His mind, like complex geometric jigsaws, struggled to connect the dots. Was this man a panther, a warrior or perhaps a modern warlock? The cold reality began to crumble his composure. He’d seen him once in a tanktop, his muscular frame hinting at a past that could easily break someone. He'd even spotted him talking on a wireless device, mentioning a 'mandatory vaccination' while his eyeball darted around like a cornered animal, hinting at illicit activities involving a private twinjet and former teammates.

One night, Arthur found himself blindfolded, a flopper in a terrifying game. He'd stumbled onto a plot to skyjack a private twinjet at the local airport, a scheme that funneled funds through various shell companies, bypassing hospitals for profit. The conspirators, an unstoppable force of individuals driven by a twisted sense of evolution, spoke in hushed tones of racketeering. He felt as light as a feather as he was pushed into a waiting droshky, a vintage ride that somehow felt more menacing. His mind raced, recalling childhood nightmares now brought to life. He realized the neighbor was a witch of an operative, her methods as serpentine as her movements, and her use of high-tech drills to access secure data. It was an elaborate trick, a felony in motion, requiring finicky precision. He felt a sudden acceleration as the vehicle sped up, and knew his own injury was imminent if he didn't act, his muscular frame tense. The mention of 'rabbits' as a code word for enzymes or some illicit substance in an upcoming 'election' further solidified the danger from these jaguars, as they passed a deserted tramway station.

Arthur emerged from the ordeal with a profound revelation. His past life of quiet contentment now seemed unbearable. He sought support in a therapist, facing his inner alien and the long-suppressed urge to truly live. He quit nicotine, started brewing kombucha, and found himself dating again, even if his attempts at forming new relationships sometimes felt labored. The weight of societal judgment no longer held his center as firmly. He pursued hobbies, joining an amateur basketball league, finding camaraderie with new teammates that felt almost sacred. One evening, while watching a Veejay spinning tracks at a local German beer garden, he felt an inner earthquake of change. He even toyed with the idea of leasing a small storefront to sell his homemade jaggery candies, using an ultrasound device to precisely cut the sweets. The old, overpacked anxieties began to osmose away. He was no longer the man haunted by a metaphorical line of ducklings always behind him, nor the specter of a nymphomaniac ex, but a truly transformed individual, leaving behind his vaporizer and embracing new delight.
rawPanelSentences: Arthur Vance, a **regular** man pushing fifty, found simple **delight** in his morning **promenade** through the **town** square, pausing to admire the ripe **plum** trees. **Obviously**, his life seemed **general** and predictable, cushioned by a steady **payroll** from his long-standing government job. He often spent **holidays** alone, yet found a peculiar **happy** contentment, allowing his **imagination** to roam. His apartment, though small, felt **wholesome**, warmed by an electric **fireplace** that offered **ease** after long days. He’d occasionally enjoy a quiet **drink**, reflecting on his past as an **overprotected** child, now a stoic man of **defence**. He wanted to **establish** a new **point** of **latitude** in his life, feeling a **hollow** spot. His past self was a bit **dorky**, the **antithesis** of the composed man he’d become, even though his home was filled with the scent of **vanilla** air freshener and his work involved mundane **keypads**.

One morning, a **rancid** smell wafted from the old apartment next door, stirring an **agony** in his gut. He remembered the cryptic **acronym** he'd seen on a package delivered there. A new, peculiar neighbor, a former **mixer** for an underground fight club, notorious for the strange **neckbands** he wore, seemed to be constantly **weathercocking** the street. Arthur, an avid **jogger**, noticed a high-speed **jetski** often docked at the nearby lake – an **undeniable** pattern emerging. He felt a metaphorical **plank** pressing on him. His mind, like complex **geometric** **jigsaws**, struggled to connect the dots. Was this man a **panther**, a **warrior** or perhaps a modern **warlock**? The **cold** reality began to **crumble** his composure. He’d seen him once in a **tanktop**, his **muscular** frame hinting at a past that could easily **break** someone. He'd even spotted him talking on a **wireless** device, mentioning a 'mandatory **vaccination**' while his **eyeball** darted around like a cornered animal, hinting at illicit activities involving a private **twinjet** and former **teammates**.

One night, Arthur found himself **blindfolded**, a **flopper** in a terrifying game. He'd stumbled onto a plot to **skyjack** a private **twinjet** at the local **airport**, a scheme that funneled funds through various shell companies, bypassing **hospitals** for profit. The conspirators, an **unstoppable** force of individuals driven by a twisted sense of **evolution**, spoke in hushed tones of **racketeering**. He felt as light as a **feather** as he was pushed into a waiting **droshky**, a vintage ride that somehow felt more menacing. His mind raced, recalling childhood **nightmares** now brought to life. He realized the neighbor was a **witch** of an operative, her methods as **serpentine** as her movements, and her use of high-tech **drills** to access secure data. It was an elaborate **trick**, a **felony** in motion, requiring **finicky** precision. He felt a sudden **acceleration** as the vehicle sped up, and knew his own **injury** was imminent if he didn't act, his **muscular** frame tense. The mention of 'rabbits' as a code word for **enzymes** or some illicit substance in an upcoming 'election' further solidified the danger from these **jaguars**, as they passed a deserted **tramway** station.

Arthur emerged from the ordeal with a profound **revelation**. His past life of quiet contentment now seemed **unbearable**. He sought **support** in a therapist, facing his inner **alien** and the long-suppressed **urge** to truly live. He quit **nicotine**, started brewing **kombucha**, and found himself **dating** again, even if his attempts at forming new relationships sometimes felt **labored**. The weight of societal **judgment** no longer held his **center** as firmly. He pursued hobbies, joining an amateur **basketball** league, finding camaraderie with new **teammates** that felt almost **sacred**. One evening, while watching a **Veejay** spinning tracks at a local **German** beer garden, he felt an inner **earthquake** of change. He even toyed with the idea of **leasing** a small storefront to sell his homemade **jaggery** candies, using an **ultrasound** device to precisely cut the sweets. The old, **overpacked** anxieties began to **osmose** away. He was no longer the man haunted by a metaphorical line of **ducklings** always behind him, nor the specter of a **nymphomaniac** ex, but a truly transformed individual, leaving behind his **vaporizer** and embracing new **delight**.
imagePrompt: A professional 4-panel comic book page, 2x2 grid, sequential art, cel-shading, bold ink outlines, cinematic lighting, thick white gutters, clean black borders. Arthur Vance, a Caucasian man in his late 50s, with a neatly trimmed gray beard and pronounced worry lines, wearing a slightly rumpled trench coat. TOP-LEFT PANEL: Arthur Vance calmly strolling on a town promenade, observing his surroundings with a thoughtful expression. Medium shot. TOP-RIGHT PANEL: Arthur Vance, his face etched with suspicion, peering intently through his apartment's peephole at a shadowy figure's door. Close-up. BOTTOM-LEFT PANEL: Arthur Vance, blindfolded and hands bound, struggling against restraints in the back of a vintage droshky, a single ray of light catching his pained face. Dutch angle, wide shot. BOTTOM-RIGHT PANEL: Arthur Vance, smiling genuinely, actively playing basketball on an outdoor court with adult teammates, a look of joyful determination on his face. Dynamic action shot, eye-level.

definitions.definition: The fruit and its tree.
Extended senses.
Of a dark bluish-red colour.
Choice; especially lavish or preferred.
definitions.example: She landed a plum position as an executive for the firm.

definitions.definition: A prom (dance).
A walk taken for pleasure, display, or exercise; a stroll.
A place where one takes a walk for leisurely pleasure, or for exercise, especially a terrace by the seaside.
A dance motion consisting of a walk, done while square dancing.
To walk for amusement, show, or exercise.
To perform the stylized walk of a square dance.
To cover the eyes, in order to make someone unable to see.
To obscure understanding or comprehension.
Wearing a blindfold
One who flops.
(skittles) The knocking down of all nine pins in one go.
A person who deliberately falls down on a slippery floor or in front of an automobile etc. so as to claim compensation.
In an obvious or clearly apparent manner.
definitions.example: She was in floods of tears and obviously very upset.

definitions.definition: Used as a filler word, or to introduce information even when not obvious.
definitions.example: You weren't to know, but I've spoken to, obviously, Jim about this, and ...

definitions.definition: To steal or commandeer (hijack) an airplane, usually by threat of violence to the passengers.
The angular distance north or south from a planet's equator, measured along the meridian of that particular point.
An imaginary line (in fact a circumference) around a planet running parallel to the planet's equator.
The relative freedom from restrictions; scope to do something.
definitions.example: His parents gave him a great deal of latitude.

definitions.definition: The angular distance of a heavenly body from the ecliptic.
The extent to which a light-sensitive material can be over- or underexposed and still achieve an acceptable result.
Extent or scope; e.g. breadth, width or amplitude.
A large medical facility, usually in a building with multiple floors, where seriously ill or injured patients are given extensive medical and/or surgical treatment.
A building founded for the long-term care of its residents, such as an almshouse. The residents may have no physical ailments, but simply need financial support.
A place of lodging.
A member of the British Army (as opposed to a member of the Territorial Army or Reserve).
A frequent, routine visitor to an establishment.
definitions.example: Bartenders usually know their regulars by name.

definitions.definition: A frequent customer, client or business partner.
definitions.example: This gentleman was one of the architect's regulars.

definitions.definition: A coffee with one cream and one sugar.
Anything that is normal or standard.
A member of a religious order who has taken the three ordinary vows.
A number for each year, giving, added to the concurrents, the number of the day of the week on which the Paschal full moon falls.
A fixed number for each month serving to ascertain the day of the week, or the age of the moon, on the first day of any month.
Bound by religious rule; belonging to a monastic or religious order (often as opposed to secular).
definitions.example: regular clergy, in distinction from the secular clergy

definitions.definition: Having a constant pattern; showing evenness of form or appearance.
(of a polygon) Both equilateral and equiangular; having all sides of the same length, and all (corresponding) angles of the same size
(of a polyhedron) Whose faces are all congruent regular polygons, equally inclined to each other.
Demonstrating a consistent set of rules; showing order, evenness of operation or occurrence.
Well-behaved, orderly; restrained (of a lifestyle etc.).
Happening at constant (especially short) intervals.
definitions.example: He made regular visits to go see his mother.

definitions.definition: (grammar, of a verb, plural, etc) Following a set or common pattern; according to the normal rules of a given language.
definitions.example: "Walked" is the past tense of the regular verb "to walk".

definitions.definition: Having the expected characteristics or appearances; normal, ordinary, standard.
Permanently organised; being part of a set professional body of troops.
Having bowel movements or menstrual periods at constant intervals in the expected way.
definitions.example: Maintaining a high-fibre diet keeps you regular.

definitions.definition: Exemplary; excellent example of; utter, downright.
definitions.example: a regular genius; a regular John Bull

definitions.definition: Having all the parts of the same kind alike in size and shape.
definitions.example: a regular flower; a regular sea urchin

definitions.definition: Isometric.
Riding with the left foot forward.
(of a Borel measure) Such that every set in its domain is both outer regular and inner regular.
Regularly, on a regular basis.
Rank in taste or smell.
definitions.example: The house was deserted, with a rancid half-eaten meal still on the dinner table.

definitions.definition: Offensive.
definitions.example: His remarks were rancid; everyone got up and left.

definitions.definition: Extreme pain.
The sufferings of Jesus Christ in the garden of Gethsemane.
Violent contest or striving.
Paroxysm of joy; keen emotion.
The last struggle of life; death struggle.

An abbreviation formed by the initial letters of other words, sometimes exclusively such abbreviations when pronounced as a word (as "laser") rather than as individual letters (initialisms such as "TNT").
An abbreviation formed by the beginning letters or syllables of other words (as "Benelux").
To form into an acronym.
One who, or a device that, mixes or merges things together.
One who mixes or socializes.
A machine outfitted with (typically blunt) blades with which it mixes or beats ingredients in a bowl below.
A non-alcoholic drink (such as lemonade, Coca-Cola or fruit juice) that is added to spirits to make cocktails.
A mixing console.
A dance or other social event meant to foster new acquaintances, as at the beginning of a school year.
Any of various social dances involving frequent changes of partners.
A device for combining hot and cold water before it emerges from a single spout or shower head.
A nonlinear electrical circuit that creates new frequencies from two signals applied to it.
A band worn around the neck.
The part of a shirt encircling the neck.
To attach a band around the neck (especially of wild animals)
A fermentation of sweetened tea of Mongolian origin.
(of a boat) To turn upwind because of the difference in water pressure on two sides.
(of an airplane or missile) To veer into the direction of the oncoming (relative) wind.
To act as a weathercock for.
A person who jogs (as exercise).
A tracksuit, particularly the trousers.
A printing press operator who removes, jogs, and stacks the sheets or signatures of paper.
(chiefly derogatory) A black person.
A powered watercraft with a seat and handlebars in a style similar to a motorbike.
To ride on a jetski.
The ball of the eye
(CB) a meeting
definitions.example: We had an eyeball last year.

definitions.definition: As a synecdoche, a person's focus of attention
(in the plural) readership or viewership
A favourite or pet; the apple of someone's eye.
To gauge, estimate or judge by eye, rather than measuring precisely; to look or glance at.
definitions.example: A good cook can often just eyeball the correct quantities of ingredients.

definitions.definition: To scrutinize
To stare at intently
definitions.example: Are you eyeballing my girl?

definitions.definition: To roll one's eyes.
Of or relating to geometry.
definitions.example: The architect used geometric techniques to design her home.

definitions.definition: Increasing or decreasing in a geometric progression.
definitions.example: Bacteria exhibit geometric increase in numbers when the environment is not limiting.

definitions.definition: Using simple shapes such as circles, triangles and lines in a decorative object.
definitions.example: The building's profile was strikingly geometric.

definitions.definition: A general fact or proposition; a generality.
definitions.example: We have dealt with the generals; now let us turn to the particulars.

definitions.definition: The holder of a senior military title, originally designating the commander of an army and now a specific rank falling under field marshal (in the British army) and below general of the army or general of the air force in the US army and air forces.
A great strategist or tactician.
definitions.example: Hannibal was one of the greatest generals of the ancient world.

definitions.definition: The head of certain religious orders, especially Dominicans or Jesuits.
A commander of naval forces; an admiral.
A general servant; a maid with no specific duties.
A general anesthetic.
General anesthesia.
The general insurance industry.
definitions.example: I work in general.

definitions.definition: To lead (soldiers) as a general.
Including or involving every part or member of a given or implied entity, whole etc.; as opposed to specific or particular.
(sometimes postpositive) Applied to a person (as a postmodifier or a normal preceding adjective) to indicate supreme rank, in civil or military titles, and later in other terms; pre-eminent.
Prevalent or widespread among a given class or area; common, usual.
Not limited in use or application; applicable to the whole or every member of a class or category.
Giving or consisting of only the most important aspects of something, ignoring minor details; indefinite.
Not limited to a specific class; miscellaneous, concerned with all branches of a given subject or area.
In a general or collective manner or sense; in most cases; upon the whole.
Irrefutable, or impossible to deny
definitions.example: Some believe that there is undeniable evidence of UFOs.

definitions.definition: A long, broad and thick piece of timber, as opposed to a board which is less thick.
A political issue that is of concern to a faction or a party of the people and the political position that is taken on that issue.
definitions.example: Germanization was a central plank of German conservative thinking in the 19th and 20th centuries.

definitions.definition: Physical exercise in which one holds a pushup position for a measured length of time.
A stupid person, idiot.
That which supports or upholds.
To cover something with planking.
definitions.example: to plank a floor or a ship

definitions.definition: To bake (fish, etc.) on a piece of cedar lumber.
To lay down, as on a plank or table; to stake or pay cash.
definitions.example: to plank money in a wager

definitions.definition: To harden, as hat bodies, by felting.
To splice together the ends of slivers of wool, for subsequent drawing.
To pose for a photograph while lying rigid, face down, arms at side, in an unusual place.
Any of various big cats with black fur; most especially, the black-coated leopard of India.
Any big cat of the genus Panthera.
A cougar; especially the Florida panther.
A creature resembling a big cat with a multicolored hide, found in Ancient Greek mythology.
A list of employees who receive salary or wages, together with the amounts due to each.
The total sum of money paid to employees.
The calculation of salaries and wages and the deduction of taxes etc.; the department in a company responsible for this.
Bribes paid to people
To place on a payroll.
The point in the interior of a circle that is equidistant from all points on the circumference.
The point in the interior of a sphere that is equidistant from all points on the circumference.
The middle portion of something; the part well away from the edges.
The point on a line that is midway between the ends.

The point in the interior of any figure of any number of dimensions that has as its coordinates the arithmetic mean of the coordinates of all points on the perimeter of the figure (or of all points in the interior for a center of volume).
The subgroup (respectively, subring), denoted Z(G), of those elements of a given group (respectively, ring) G that commute with every element of G.
A place where the greater part of some function or activity occurs.
definitions.example: shopping center, convention center, civic center, garment center, Lincoln Center for the Performing Arts, Rockefeller Center

definitions.definition: A topic that is particularly important in a given context.
definitions.example: the center of attention

definitions.definition: A player in the middle of the playing area
A player who can go all over the court, except the shooting circles.
A pass played into the centre of the pitch.
One of the backs operating in a central area of the pitch, either the inside centre or outside centre.
A temporary structure upon which the materials of a vault or arch are supported in position until the work becomes self-supporting.
One of the two conical steel pins in a lathe, etc., upon which the work is held, and about which it revolves.
A conical recess or indentation in the end of a shaft or other work, to receive the point of a center, on which the work can turn, as in a lathe.
The ensemble of moderate or centrist political parties.
To cause (an object) to occupy the center of an area.
definitions.example: He centered the heading of the document.

definitions.definition: To cause (some attribute, such as a mood or voltage) to be adjusted to a value which is midway between the extremes.
To give (something) a central basis.
To concentrate on (something), to pay close attention to (something).
definitions.example: The discussion centered around the recent issues.

definitions.definition: To form a recess or indentation for the reception of a center.
Of, at, or related to a center.
A sport in which two opposing teams of five players strive to put a ball through a hoop.
The particular kind of ball used in the sport of basketball.
An aircraft powered by two jet engines.
Two jets of particles from an event or source
One who is on the same team.
An item of casual clothing: a top with no sleeves.
The top of a ship's bilge tank, usually the lowest horizontal surface on board.
A settlement; an area with residential districts, shops and amenities, and its own local government; especially one larger than a village and smaller than a city.
definitions.example: This town is really dangerous because these youngsters have Beretta handguns.

definitions.definition: Any more urbanized center than the place of reference.
definitions.example: I'll be in Yonkers, then I'm driving into town to see the Knicks at the Garden tonight.

definitions.definition: A rural settlement in which a market was held at least once a week.
The residents (as opposed to gown: the students, faculty, etc.) of a community which is the site of a university.
Used to refer to a town or similar entity under discussion.
definitions.example: Call me when you get to town.

definitions.definition: A major city, especially one where the speaker is located.
A municipal organization, such as a corporation, defined by the laws of the entity of which it is a part.
An enclosure which surrounded the mere homestead or dwelling of the lord of the manor.
The whole of the land which constituted the domain.
A collection of houses enclosed by fences or walls.
A farm or farmstead; also, a court or farmyard.
To protect to an excessive degree; to coddle
(sometimes attributive) Something which supports.
definitions.example: Don't move that beam! It's a support for the whole platform.

definitions.definition: Financial or other help.
definitions.example: The government provides support to the arts in several ways.

definitions.definition: Answers to questions and resolution of problems regarding something sold.
definitions.example: Sure they sell the product, but do they provide support?

definitions.definition: In relation to a function, the set of points where the function is not zero, or the closure of that set.
(fuzzy set theory) A set whose elements are at least partially included in a given fuzzy set (i.e., whose grade of membership in that fuzzy set is strictly greater than zero).
definitions.example: If the membership function of a fuzzy set is continuous, then that fuzzy set's support is an open set.

definitions.definition: Evidence.
definitions.example: The new research provides further support for our theory.

definitions.definition: Compatibility and functionality for a given product or feature.
definitions.example: This game has no mouse support.

definitions.definition: An actor playing a subordinate part with a star.
An accompaniment in music.
(structural analysis) Horizontal, vertical or rotational support of structures: movable, hinged, fixed.
To keep from falling.
definitions.example: Don’t move that beam! It supports the whole platform.

definitions.definition: To answer questions and resolve problems regarding something sold.
definitions.example: Sure they sell the product, but do they support it?

definitions.definition: To back a cause, party, etc., mentally or with concrete aid.
definitions.example: I support France in the World Cup.

definitions.definition: To help, particularly financially.
definitions.example: The government supports the arts in several ways.

definitions.definition: To verify; to make good; to substantiate; to establish; to sustain.
definitions.example: The evidence will not support the statements or allegations.

definitions.definition: To serve, as in a customer-oriented mindset; to give support to.
definitions.example: I don't make decisions, but I support those who do.

definitions.definition: To be designed (said of machinery, electronics, or computers, or their parts, accessories, peripherals, or programming) to function compatibly with or provide the capacity for.
definitions.example: Early personal computers did not support voice-recognition hardware or software.

definitions.definition: To be accountable for, or involved with, but not responsible for.
definitions.example: I support the administrative activities of the executive branch of the organization.

definitions.definition: To endure without being overcome; bear; undergo; to tolerate.
To assume and carry successfully, as the part of an actor; to represent or act; to sustain.
definitions.example: to support the character of King Lear

definitions.definition: A position in which a gymnast is holding their body above the rings (or high bar, parallel bars...) usually with straight arms.
A day on which a festival, religious event, or national celebration is traditionally observed.
definitions.example: Today is a Wiccan holiday!

definitions.definition: A day declared free from work by the state or government.
A period of one or more days taken off work for leisure and often travel; often plural.
A period during which pupils do not attend their school; often plural; rarely used for students at university (usually: vacation).
definitions.example: I want to take a French course this summer holiday.

definitions.definition: A period during which, by agreement, the usual payments are not made.
definitions.example: a mortgage payment holiday

definitions.definition: A gap in coverage, e.g. of paint on a surface, or sonar imagery.
To take a period of time away from work or study.
To spend a period of time for travel.
(usually after possessive) holiday, vacation; time off work, especially if spent travelling away from home
The season around Christmas, Hanukkah, and New Year's Day.
definitions.example: Where will you be spending the holidays?

definitions.definition: A happy event, thing, person, etc.
Preceded by the: happy people as a group.
Often followed by up: to become happy; to brighten up, to cheer up.
Often followed by up: to make happy; to brighten, to cheer, to enliven.
Having a feeling arising from a consciousness of well-being or of enjoyment; enjoying good of any kind, such as comfort, peace, or tranquillity; blissful, contented, joyous.
definitions.example: Music makes me feel happy.

definitions.definition: Experiencing the effect of favourable fortune; favored by fortune or luck; fortunate, lucky, propitious.
Content, satisfied (with or to do something); having no objection (to something).
definitions.example: Are you happy to pay me back by the end of the week?

definitions.definition: (Of acts, speech, etc.) Appropriate, apt, felicitous.
definitions.example: a happy coincidence

definitions.definition: (in combination) Favoring or inclined to use.
definitions.example: slaphappy, trigger-happy

definitions.definition: (of people, often followed by "at" or "in") Dexterous, ready, skilful.
The act of revealing or disclosing.
Something that is revealed.
Something dramatically disclosed.
A manifestation of divine truth.
A great success.
The image-making power of the mind; the act of mentally creating or reproducing an object not previously perceived; the ability to create such images.
definitions.example: Imagination is one of the most advanced human faculties.

definitions.definition: Particularly, construction of false images; fantasizing.
definitions.example: You think someone's been following you? That's just your imagination.

definitions.definition: Creativity; resourcefulness.
definitions.example: His imagination makes him a valuable team member.

definitions.definition: A mental image formed by the action of the imagination as a faculty; something imagined.
An alkaloid (C10H14N2), commonly occurring in the tobacco plant. In small doses it is a habit-forming stimulant; in larger doses it is toxic and is often used in insecticides.
Tobacco, cigarettes
definitions.example: He is addicted to nicotine.

definitions.definition: To note the time or place of writing or executing; to express in an instrument the time of its execution.

definitions.definition: To note or fix the time of (an event); to give the date of.
To determine the age of something.

definitions.definition: To take (someone) on a date, or a series of dates.
(by extension) To have a steady relationship with; to be romantically involved with.
(by extension) To have a steady relationship with each other; to be romantically involved with each other.
definitions.example: They met a couple of years ago, but have been dating for about five months.

definitions.definition: To make or become old, especially in such a way as to fall out of fashion, become less appealing or attractive, etc.

definitions.definition: (with from) To have beginning; to begin; to be dated or reckoned.
A form of romantic courtship typically between two individuals with the aim of assessing the other's suitability as a partner in an intimate relationship or as a spouse.
An estimation of the age of an artifact, biological vestige, linguistic usage, etc.
The setting of a date on which an event or transaction is to take place or take effect.
A young duck.
The medium of radio communication.

definitions.definition: A radio set.

definitions.definition: To send a message by wireless (by radio)
Not having any wires.
Of or relating to communication without a wired connection, such as by radio waves.
A person who is actively engaged in battle, conflict or warfare; a soldier or combatant.
A person who is aggressively, courageously, or energetically involved in an activity, such as athletics.
Promoting good physical health and well-being.
Promoting moral and mental well-being.
Favourable to morals, religion or prosperity; sensible; conducive to good; salutary; promoting virtue or being virtuous.
Marked by wholeness; sound and healthy.
A male magic-user; a male witch.
A saw with fine teeth and a narrow blade which can cut curves in wood or metal.
A jigsaw puzzle.

A change of position.
An unfolding.
Process of development.
Unable to be stopped.

definitions.definition: (of a thing) Having a low temperature.

definitions.definition: (of the weather) Causing the air to be cold.

definitions.definition: (of a person or animal) Feeling the sensation of coldness, especially to the point of discomfort.

definitions.definition: Unfriendly, emotionally distant or unfeeling.

definitions.definition: Dispassionate, not prejudiced or partisan, impartial.

definitions.definition: Completely unprepared; without introduction.

definitions.definition: Unconscious or deeply asleep; deprived of the metaphorical heat associated with life or consciousness.

definitions.definition: (usually with "have" or "know" transitively) Perfectly, exactly, completely; by heart.

definitions.definition: (usually with "have" transitively) Cornered, done for.

definitions.definition: Not pungent or acrid.
Unexciting; dull; uninteresting.
Affecting the sense of smell (as of hunting dogs) only feebly; having lost its odour.

definitions.definition: Not sensitive; not acute.
Distant; said, in the game of hunting for some object, of a seeker remote from the thing concealed. Compare warm and hot.

definitions.definition: Having a bluish effect; not warm in colour.
Rarely used or accessed, and thus able to be relegated to slower storage.

definitions.definition: Inoculation with a vaccine, in order to protect from a particular disease or strain of disease.
Something that breaks.

A user of CB radio.

A branching, hair-like structure that grows on the bodies of birds, used for flight, swimming, protection and display.
Long hair on the lower legs of a dog or horse, especially a draft horse, notably the Clydesdale breed. Narrowly only the rear hair.
One of the fins or wings on the shaft of an arrow.
A longitudinal strip projecting from an object to strengthen it, or to enter a channel in another object and thereby prevent displacement sideways but permit motion lengthwise; a spline.
Kind; nature; species (from the proverbial phrase "birds of a feather").

One of the two shims of the three-piece stone-splitting tool known as plug and feather or plug and feathers; the feathers are placed in a borehole and then a wedge is driven between them, causing the stone to split.
The angular adjustment of an oar or paddle-wheel float, with reference to a horizontal axis, as it leaves or enters the water.
Anything petty or trifling; a whit or jot.
(in the plural) Partridges and pheasants, as opposed to rabbits and hares (called fur).
To cover or furnish with feathers.
To arrange in the manner or appearance of feathers.

To streamline the blades of an aircraft's propeller by rotating them perpendicular to the axis of the propeller when the engine is shut down so that the propeller does not windmill during flight.

To be nurtured or trained up together.

definitions.example: a man of judgment

definitions.definition: The conclusion or result of judging; an opinion; a decision.
The act of determining, as in courts of law, what is conformable to law and justice; also, the determination, decision, or sentence of a court, or of a judge.
The final award; the last sentence.
To make stable or firm; to confirm.
To form; to found; to institute; to set up in business.
To appoint or adopt, as officers, laws, regulations, guidelines, etc.; to enact; to ordain.
To prove and cause to be accepted as true; to establish a fact; to demonstrate.
A shaking of the ground, caused by volcanic activity or movement around geologic faults.
(planetary geology) Such a quake specifically occurring on the planet Earth, as opposed to other celestial bodies.
A strong desire; an itch to do something.
To press; to push; to drive; to impel; to force onward.
To press the mind or will of; to ply with motives, arguments, persuasion, or importunity.
To provoke; to exasperate.
To press hard upon; to follow closely.
To present in an urgent manner; to insist upon.
definitions.example: to urge an argument; to urge the necessity of a case

definitions.definition: To treat with forcible means; to take severe or violent measures with.
definitions.example: to urge an ore with intense heat

definitions.definition: To press onward or forward.
To be pressing in argument; to insist; to persist.
So unpleasant or painful as to be unendurable
